data.title: No probability of an upcoming recession, according to the several metrics – analyst
data.description:
The probability of a recession is quite low, maybe at zero, said Jitesh Kumar, derivatives strategist at Societe Generale, in a note.

In early August, the U.S. rates market was starting to fully price a recession. That could have been an overreaction, said Kumar.

“Economic data has indeed improved since then, and the rates market is less pessimistic today.”

Equity volatility, as measured by the VIX Index (VIX), continues to be significantly lower than what was implied in the rates market.

The index is up 56.47% year-to-date, but down 4.74% from the past five days.

“While VIX (VIX) at [about] 20 seems high considering recent history, it translates to less than 10% Bayesian probability of recession,” he said, refereeing to data going back to 1990.

“Interestingly, U.S. high yield spreads imply a zero percent chance of recession,” according to data going back to 1987, he said.

The U.S. High Yield Master II Option-Adjusted Spread is at 2.9%, vs. 2.94% the previous market day, and 4.25% last year.

The Invesco Fundamental High Yield Corporate Bond ETF (PHB) is up 1.65% year-to-date, and down 0.81% from the past month.

Other high yield ETFs: (HYG), (JNK), (VYM).

data.title: Nordea Bank Abp GAAP EPS of EUR 0.36
data.description:
• Nordea Bank Abp press release (OTCQX:NRDBY): Q3 GAAP EPS of EUR 0.36
  • Operating profit amounted to EUR 1.6bn.
  • Total income was up 2%. Net interest income was 1% lower, following policy rate reductions. Net fee and commission income continued to grow, up 4%. Net insurance result remained solid and net fair value result improved by 26%.
  • Costs rose by 9%, excluding a EUR 32m item related to the settlement of a regulatory investigation in the US
  • Net loan losses and similar net result amounted to EUR 51m or 6bp.
  • Updated outlook and now expect full-year 2024 return on equity to be above 16%.
  • Nordea’s financial target for 2025 is a return on equity of above 15%.

data.title: Sealed Air double-upgraded at Raymond James
data.description:
• Sealed Air (NYSE:SEE) on Thursday was double-upgraded to Strong Buy from a previous investment rating of Market Perform by analysts at financial-services firm Raymond James. They said the maker of Bubble Wrap and other packaging is poised for gains with the introduction of new products and realigning its businesses for higher margins.
  • Sealed Air’s (SEE) profit margin in food packaging likely is being overlooked by investors, according to Raymond James.
  • Shares of Sealed Air (SEE) rose 2.6% in the first couple of hours of regular trading on Thursday.

data.title: TSMC CFO expects 2025 capex 'very likely' to outpace 2024
data.description:
Taiwan Manufacturing Company (NYSE:TSM) expects its capital expenditures to increase in 2025 after a hefty 2024 due to artificial intelligence-related demand.

"As the strong, structural AI-related demand continues, we continue to invest to support our customers' growth," said TSMC Chief Financial Officer Wendell Huang, during the company's third quarter 2024 earnings call on Thursday. "We now expect our 2024 capex to be slightly higher than $30B U.S. dollars. Between 70% and 80% of the capital budget will be allocated for advanced process technologies."

While Huang could not yet provide a capex number for 2025, he indicated it is increasing. He said they can provide more insight on this in January.

"For TSMC, a higher level of capital expenditures is always going to be related with higher growth opportunities in the coming years," Huang said. "And as long as our growth outlook remains strong, we will continue to invest. So it is very likely that our capex next year will be higher than this year."

Higher capex in 2025 could benefit semiconductor equipment makers, such as Lam Research (NASDAQ:LRCX), ASML Holding (NASDAQ:ASML), KLA (NASDAQ:KLAC) and Applied Materials (NASDAQ:AMAT).

Applied Materials edged up about 2% during Thursday morning trading while Lam Research ticked up 0.5%. ASML and KLA were relatively flat.

ASML has dropped 20% this week, following its third quarter results. ASML noted a likely decline in the sale of advanced semiconductor manufacturing equipment to China due to new export regulations in the Netherlands.

TSM was surging 13% following its latest financial results, released pre-market on Thursday.

data.title: Crude inventory fell 2.2M barrels for week ended Oct. 11 - EIA
data.description:
Commercial crude stocks for the week ended October. 11: 804.4M barrels.

Crude inventory change: -2.2M barrels vs. 5.8M barrels for the week ended Oct. 4. Consensus estimate 1.800M.

Gasoline inventory change: -2.2M barrels vs. -6.3M barrels for the week ended Oct. 4. Consensus estimate -1.400M.

Futures (CL1:COM) +0.76% to $70.92.

ETFs: (USO), (UCO), (SCO), (BNO), (DBO), (USL).

data.title: ABB raises margin guidance on strength in electrification business
data.description:
ABB (OTCPK:ABBNY) +2.6% in Thursday's trading despite missing Q3 GAAP earnings and revenue expectations, as the company slightly raised its full-year margin guidance, supported by strength in its electrification business that more than offset weakness in robotics and discrete automation.

Q3 net profit rose 7.3% Y/Y to $947M on revenues that rose 2.4% on a comparable basis to $8.15B, while operational EBITDA jumped 12% to $1.55B and margin increased to 19% from 17% in year-earlier quarter; in the electrification division, Q3 operational EBITDA margin reached 24% for the first time, and revenues rose 10% to a new high of $3.91B.

Analysts expected ABB (OTCPK:ABBNY) to report revenues of $8.34B, operational EBITDA of $1.52B, and net profit of $981M, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

Q3 orders edged higher to $8.19B from $8.05B for the prior-year period, in line with anticipated orders of $8.18B, according to consensus estimates provided by Visible Alpha.

For the full year, ABB (OTCPK:ABBNY) raised its margin guidance, now forecasting operational EBITDA margin at slightly above 18% after previously guiding for ~18%.

For Q4, ABB (OTCPK:ABBNY) said it expects low to mid-single digit comparable revenue growth and a sequentially lower operational EBITDA margin.

data.title: Getlink reports Q3 results
data.description:
• Getlink press release (OTCPK:GRPTY): Q3 Revenue of €475M.

  • Europorte: revenue up 5% to €40 million
  • Target EBITDA for 2024 between €780 and €830 million confirmed.

data.title: SentinelOne, AWS expand AI-based cybersecurity pact
data.description:
SentinelOne (NYSE:S) has expanded its strategic collaboration with Amazon Web Services (NASDAQ:AMZN) for AI-powered cybersecurity.

Under the partnership, SentinelOne will use Amazon Bedrock AI infrastructure to power its Purple AI cybersecurity analyst service. The expanded deal will also bring SentinelOne’s entire Singularity AI platform within AWS Marketplace.

Purple AI helps security teams detect and respond to cyberthreats earlier and faster by converting natural language questions into structured queries to guide analysts through investigations, summarizing event logs and auto-generating communications.
